exemplar.title: Which London Premier League Football Club should I support? Using Voronoi Diagrams.

detailedExemplarData.title: Which London Premier League Football Club should I support? Using Voronoi Diagrams.

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Logical chronological sequence with clear development of each mathematical step
Well-structured document with contents page, headings and embedded figures
Title and sections effectively communicate context and aim
weaknesses: Verbose narrative with repeated explanations and digressions
Formatting inconsistencies (spacing, indentation) and occasional grammatical errors
Some captions and appendix material are poorly placed or incomplete
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is coherent and organized into a clear introduction, body, and conclusion, matching band 3 descriptors. Minor awkward prose, verbosity, and formatting issues prevent a higher score, but overall the work remains logically developed and navigable.

strengths: Correct use of key mathematical terms (perpendicular bisector, largest empty circle)
Effective integration of equations, tables, diagrams and Desmos graphs
Completed Voronoi diagram demonstrates clear multiple representations
weaknesses: Inconsistent notation and decimal places; missing axis labels and scale on graphs
Typographical errors and occasional misuse of terms (e.g. âellipse graphâ for circle)
Lack of error analysis and inconsistent rounding methods hinder clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While mathematical language and representations are present and generally appropriate, inconsistencies in notation, labelling omissions, and clarity lapses align most closely with band 2 overall for mathematical communication.

strengths: Investigation driven by a personally meaningful scenario (the brothersâ dilemma)
Clear choice of context and scale that reflects independent thinking
Creative extensions (population density and transport overlays) demonstrate initiative
weaknesses: Rationale for site selection (only seven clubs) is not fully justified
Personal narrative, though engaging, could be more deeply integrated into mathematical decisions
Opportunities for greater originality (e.g. alternative scenarios) are unexploited
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows significant evidence of independent thinking, a clear personal approach, and creative initiative, fitting band 2 descriptors. It stops short of the depth and originality required for the top band.

strengths: Acknowledges measurement and rounding errors and notes difference between straight-line and actual travel distances
Includes brief critique of circle size differences in conclusion
weaknesses: Reflection is shallow and descriptive rather than analytical
Lacks discussion of model limitations (e.g. Euclidean vs. network distances) and no exploration of extensions
Minimal critical evaluation of methods or implications
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is present but limited to surface-level observations and basic critique, corresponding to band 1. The work lacks the meaningful and critical reflection required for higher bands.

strengths: Appropriate use of course-level mathematics (perpendicular bisectors, distance formula, Pythagoras)
Clear presentation of scale conversion and construction of the largest empty circle
Demonstrates a sound understanding of Voronoi concepts and applies them effectively
weaknesses: Arithmetic and algebraic errors in bisector equations and intercept calculations
Inconsistent decimal accuracy and missing explicit unit-conversion formula
Limited discussion of conceptual oversights (e.g. road vs. straight-line distance)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is relevant and at the SL course level, showing good understanding in parts. However, noticeable computational errors and lack of rigor in some explanations keep the work within band 3 rather than higher bands.

strandSelections.reasoning: Selecting a personally motivated scenario, determining a scale, and extending the basic Voronoi idea to the Largest Empty Circle show that the student is not merely copying a textbook procedure.  These choices constitute significant evidence of independent thinking, though they stop short of the depth required for outstanding originality.

The student shows a sound grasp of how perpendicular bisectors create Voronoi edges and how vertices relate to empty circles, indicating good understanding in parts of the exploration.  Some conceptual oversights (e.g. equating small measurement differences directly to kilometres, limited discussion of road distance vs. Euclidean) keep the work from âthoroughâ understanding.

Calculations of gradients, mid-points and radii are mostly correct, but there are noticeable slips: the sign of the first gradient, inconsistent decimal accuracy, and an unexplained jump from unit distances to real kilometres in one table.  These errors reduce reliability, so the mathematics is only partially correct.

Most of the material (definitions, construction steps, tables, maps) is relevant to answering the two driving questions, yet the narrative is verbose.  Lengthy anecdotal passages, repeated explanations of âas the crow fliesâ, and screenshots duplicated in different sections add non-essential text, so the exploration is mostlyâbut not fullyâconcise.

Mathematical terminology such as âperpendicular bisectorâ, âgradientâ, âdomainâ, âverticesâ, âlargest empty circleâ is used correctly most of the time, and symbolic notation for coordinates and line equations is largely accurate.  Minor slips (e.g. missing absolute value in distance, inconsistent decimal places) prevent full consistency, but usage is mostly appropriate throughout.

The exposition is generally readable, but inconsistencies in rounding and occasional typographical errors (e.g. âwhereâ vs. âwereâ) cause small lapses in clarity.  Mathematical symbols sometimes change style between lines, so the communication is mostly clear with some inconsistencies, aligning with band 2.

Perpendicular bisectors, distance formula, scale conversion and Largest Empty Circle are all at the appropriate SL level and are used throughout the exploration.  The mathematics addresses the stated aims directly, so it is relevant and at course level, though not especially deep or sophisticated.

The student combines algebraic equations, Desmos graphs, labelled diagrams, tables and annotated maps.  These representations complement each other (equations justify the edges, diagrams visualise them, tables summarise radii), demonstrating effective multiple representation, though not an especially sophisticated integration deserving the top band.

Reflection is present but brief.  The student notes a possible 0.04 unit measurement error and recognises rounding issues, yet the discussion is surface-level and does not explore implications or alternative methods.  Hence there is limited reflection only.

Overlaying population density and transport maps onto the Voronoi output and computing a scale factor to convert units to kilometres display notable initiative and some creativity.  The techniques, however, remain within standard extensions of Voronoi analysis, so the level is âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ.

The investigation is arranged in a logical chronological sequence: introduction â aim â construction steps â analysis â conclusion.  Each new mathematical step (choice of scale, perpendicular bisectors, domain restrictions, Largest Empty Circle) follows sensibly from the previous one and the reader can follow the argument without getting lost.  While the prose is sometimes awkward and several digressions interrupt the flow, the overall line of reasoning remains clear; therefore the work is coherent with clear logical development but not polished enough for the highest band.

Some basic critique appearsâe.g. acknowledging that âas the crow fliesâ differs from walking distance and comparing the two largest circlesâbut the analysis is largely descriptive.  Deeper evaluation of model validity or parameter sensitivity is absent, so the work fits the âbasic analysis with limited critiqueâ description.

The conclusion reiterates how the Voronoi diagram helped identify a club and a potential new stadium site, demonstrating a limited but visible link between reflection and mathematical understanding.  The enhancement to understanding is minimal, therefore band 1 is appropriate.

A contents page, labelled sections, headings and sub-headings provide a discernible introduction, body and conclusion.  Figures are embedded close to the text they illustrate and tables (e.g. domains, largest empty circle) summarise results effectively.  Transitions are generally clear, though placement of some screenshots is uneven and the appendix material is not clearly separated; nevertheless, the organisation meets the descriptors for being well organised with a clear three-part structure.

The exploration is clearly driven by the studentâs own context (the brothersâ dilemma and a hypothetical new club).  This personal story frames each mathematical decision, giving the work a clear personal approach that guides the investigation, but the narrative is not developed enough to be âhighly personalâ.

annotations.comment: The population density overlay demonstrates initiative but lacks axis labels and scale information; include these for full mathematical communication.
The coordinate text is cut off mid-sentence, reducing clarity; ensure complete sentences and full figure captions.
The scale conversion is central but presented without an explicit formula; include the unit-conversion equation to strengthen the argument.
The calculation of the gradient m=1.911 appears correct, but the sign convention should be explicitly justified.
The âFurther Constructionâ header is redundant here; integrate with prior text or label as a subsection to avoid repetition.
The title effectively communicates the investigation topic and method, providing clear context for the reader.
The metadata block is crowded and interrupts the flow; consider placing candidate and word-count details separately or in a header to improve readability.
The contents page clearly lists sections and page numbers, aiding navigation.
The spacing and indentation in the Table of Contents are inconsistent; refine formatting to maintain a polished, professional layout.
There is a grammatical error (âwhere both Americanâ); revise to âwho were both Americanâ to improve clarity and correctness.
The aim is clearly stated and connects well to the context, guiding the investigation appropriately.
Consider refining the aim to include specific measurable objectives (e.g. accuracy of distance calculations) to increase rigor.
The definition of âSitesâ is descriptive but would benefit from a formal definition, perhaps accompanied by a diagram with labelled axes.
The description of vertices is slightly imprecise; clarify that vertices are equidistant intersections of perpendicular bisectors.
Site selection of only seven clubs lacks justification; include rationale for excluding lower-division or peripheral sites.
When finding the negative reciprocal, include a brief statement of why mâ¥=â1/m to reinforce conceptual understanding.
The y-intercept in the bisector equation should be recalculated; 63.225â0.523Â·57.400â93.245, not 93.078, indicating an arithmetic error.
Outline more clearly how the perpendicular bisector functions as a Voronoi edge, perhaps illustrating domain restrictions on axes.
The narrative on restricting domains is verbose and would benefit from a concise, step-by-step bullet list or diagram.
The table lists many domains but uses inconsistent notation (underlined x); standardize to a single style for clarity.
The opening paragraph on bisectors is lengthy and repeats previous points; consider condensing to maintain conciseness.
The completed Voronoi diagram is visually clear and shows effective integration of representations.
Include a brief reflection on the modelâs limitation (Euclidean vs. actual travel distance) immediately under the figure.
The conclusion that the brothers should support Chelsea is concise but could be strengthened by showing their home coordinate on the diagram.
The Largest Empty Circle section is well introduced, but the term âellipse graphâ is incorrect; it should be a circle graph.
The description of the circleâs purpose is thorough but could be more concise; consider focusing on key mathematical properties.
The vertex selection for radius calculation is well documented and shows clear multiple representations.
The Pythagoras demonstration is accurate and clearly presented, reinforcing understanding.
Consider discussing how rounding of distances affects the Largest Empty Circle ranking, as the 0.04-unit difference is critical.
The unit-conversion and rounding step should include error analysis to justify reducing to three decimal places.
The summary table effectively consolidates data, but the column âReal life Distance (km) (3 dp.)â needs a note on the rounding method used.
The area difference calculation is correct, but its significance (3000 kmÂ²) should be interpreted in context of Londonâs total area.
The computed area difference is stated with excessive precision; round to an appropriate number of significant figures.
The overlap with population density is a valuable extension, demonstrating effective integration of representations.
The statement about higher population density in circle 4 is qualitative; include actual population figures or density metrics.
The transport overlay insight shows creativity and initiative, extending the modelâs applicability.
Quantify the transport advantage by counting stations within each circle and comparing connectivity metrics.
The conclusion restates objectives well but lacks critical reflection on limitations and possible extensions.
The reflective survey of friends and family is interesting; include sample size and methods to support the validity of this informal test.
The personal narrative of the brothersâ dilemma is engaging and drives the exploration effectively.
